note: Lazy-load exposes a text-format filter, image field formatters, a single lazy.settings config object, a `lazy` service, and four alter hooks. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want images and iframes pasted into a rich-text Body field (Full HTML format) to lazy-load. Which Lazy-load filter do I enable and where?
evals.expected_output: Enable the 'Lazy-load images and iframes' filter (id lazy_filter) on the text format at Admin > Config > Content authoring > Text formats and editors; its image/iframe checkboxes pick which tags.

evals.prompt: How do I make the Lazy module use the browser's native loading="lazy" attribute instead of loading the lazysizes JavaScript library? Which setting controls this?
evals.expected_output: Enable the preferNative setting on lazy.settings: when true the module adds loading="lazy" and no lazysizes JS runs; when false (default) lazysizes is used for all browsers.

evals.prompt: I want one specific image to NOT be lazy-loaded by the Lazy module. What is the mechanism and the default class name?
evals.expected_output: Add the skipClass (default 'no-lazy') to the element (or its parent); Lazy skips any element whose classes contain the skipClass.

evals.prompt: How do I enable lazy-loading for an image field in a particular view mode on a Drupal site using the Lazy module?
evals.expected_output: In Manage display choose the 'Image (Lazy-load)' (lazy_image) or 'Responsive image (Lazy-load)' formatter, or check the 'Enable lazy-loading' box Lazy adds to the image/responsive_image/colorbox/media_thumbnail formatters via third-party settings.

evals.prompt: What is the settings route and path for the Lazy-load module's configuration form, and which permission gates it?
evals.expected_output: Route lazy.config_form at /admin/config/content/lazy, gated by the core 'administer filters' permission.

evals.prompt: In the Lazy module, how do I stop lazy-loading from running on the RSS feed path, and how would I instead limit it to only certain pages? Which config key holds this?
evals.expected_output: The visibility key on lazy.settings uses the request_path condition (default excludes /rss.xml). negate=0 means everywhere except listed pages; negate=1 limits lazy-loading to only the listed pages.

evals.prompt: My custom module provides an image-based field formatter and I want Lazy-load to offer its lazy-loading option on it. Which hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_lazy_field_formatters_alter(array &$formatters) and append your formatter id to $formatters.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal site, which service and method tell me whether Lazy-loading is allowed for the current request path?
evals.expected_output: Use the 'lazy' service (\Drupal::service('lazy'), Drupal\lazy\Lazy); call isPathAllowed() (or isEnabled()) which returns false on AMP pages, admin routes when disable_admin, and paths excluded by the visibility condition.
